title: CARB Holds LCFS 2024 Annual Reporting Kickoff Webinar: Possibility of Early 2025 Effective Date for LCFS Amendments Remains Open
content: At the 2024 Annual Reporting Kickoff Webinar held by CARB on 4 March, Greg Mayeur, Chief, Program Planning and Management Branch, overseeing the LCFS verification program, addressed a common question regarding the Office of Administrative Law (OAL) disapproval and the effective date of recent regulatory amendments. Mayeur explained that a target effective date has not yet been established, as they are currently reviewing the OAL decision and preparing modifications for a 15-day public comment period. The timeline for the effective date will depend on how quickly revisions can be submitted and approved by the OAL. Greg Mayeur indicated that the possibility of a January 2025 effective date remains open in the event that the updated LCFS regulation is approved by the OAL before June 30, 2025 which is the QFTR (Quarterly Fuel Transactions Report) submission deadline for Q1 2025. In the event that OAL approval takes longer than June 30 2025, the updated regulation could come into effect either mid-year 2025 or Q1 2026, subject to legalities. Until the amended regulation takes effect, the July 2020 version of the LCFS would remain effective, including applying the CA GREET 3.0 model and the related Tier 1 and 2 calculators for carbon intensity calculations. For more details on the developments surrounding OAL's disapproval and the underlying reasons, read cCarbon's in-depth articles linked below: https://www.ccarbon.info/article/carb-outlines-swift-action-plan-drawing-on-past-experience-to-address-oals-decision-of-disapproval/?tp=ca-lcfs-dashboard&lp=ccarbon-reports-market-analysis https://www.ccarbon.info/article/california-lcfs-changes-in-limbo-after-oal-disapproval-effective-date-remains-uncertain/?tp=ca-lcfs-dashboard&lp=ccarbon-reports-market-analysis
author: Analysts Account
